verses.1: For the music director; on the Gittith. A Psalm of Asaph. Sing for joy to God our strength; Shout joyfully to the God of Jacob.
verses.2: Raise a song, strike the tambourine, The sweet sounding lyre with the harp.
verses.3: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, At the full moon, on our feast day.
verses.4: For it is a statute for Israel, An ordinance of the God of Jacob.
verses.5: He established it as a testimony in Joseph When he went throughout the land of Egypt. I heard a language I did not know:
verses.6: “I relieved his shoulder of the burden, His hands were freed from the basket.
verses.7: “You called in trouble and I rescued you; I answered you in the hiding place of thunder; I put you to the test at the waters of Meribah. Selah
verses.8: “Hear, My people, and I will admonish you; Israel, if you would listen to Me!
verses.9: “There shall be no strange god among you; Nor shall you worship a foreign god.
verses.10: “I, the LORD, am your God, Who brought you up from the land of Egypt; Open your mouth wide and I will fill it.
verses.11: “But My people did not listen to My voice, And Israel did not obey Me.
verses.12: “So I gave them over to the stubbornness of their heart, To walk by their own plans.
verses.13: “Oh that My people would listen to Me, That Israel would walk in My ways!
verses.14: “I would quickly subdue their enemies And turn My hand against their adversaries.
verses.15: “Those who hate the LORD would pretend to obey Him, And their time of punishment would be forever.
verses.16: “But I would feed you with the finest of the wheat, And with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”
